source_evidence: Appendix B.2, Eq. (17)-(19): n = AB x AC; t_w = (PA.n)/(d.n); W = P + t_w d; triple products V1 = [d PC PB], V2 = [d PA PC], V3 = [d PB PA]; 'segment PQ intersects triangle ABC iff 0 <= t_w <= 1 and sign(V1)=sign(V2)=sign(V3).'
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects a flat triangular restriction primitive, by intersecting the segment with the triangle's plane and using signed triple products to check the point is inside the triangle.
assumptions: Triangle is flat, defined by points A, B, C
Point W is inside the triangle if it is on the same side of all edges (works for clockwise or counterclockwise orientation)
Segment parameterized as X = P + td, 0 <= t <= 1
formula_or_algorithm: d = Q - P. Plane normal n = AB x AC. Intersection parameter t_w = (PA . n)/(d . n); W = P + t_w d. Triple products V1 = [d, PC, PB], V2 = [d, PA, PC], V3 = [d, PB, PA]. The segment intersects triangle ABC iff 0 <= t_w <= 1 AND sign(V1) = sign(V2) = sign(V3).
validation_opportunities: unit tests: segment through interior, through an edge/vertex, parallel to plane, missing the triangle
orientation independence check (CW and CCW triangles)
degenerate case d.n = 0 (segment parallel to plane)

reuse_risk.explanation: Standard segment-triangle intersection via triple products; robust apart from the parallel (d.n=0) degeneracy.

missing_information: Handling of the d.n = 0 (segment coplanar/parallel) case
Tie-breaking when a triple product is exactly zero (on an edge)
